title: WS5: Dead Code Removal
description: Remove dead schema fields and unused compatibility code that create a misleading API surface.
close_reason: All child tasks completed

title: Remove dead schema fields
description:
**Objective:** Remove unused schema fields from `OcBlackbytesConfigSchema` that have no consumers.

**Context:** The schema at `config/schema/oc-blackbytes-config.ts:44-57` includes fields that were never implemented: `disabled_hooks` (line 48), `mcp_env_alllowlist` (line 50, also a typo), `auto_update` (line 53), `_migrations` (line 57).

**Scope:**
- IN: Verify schema uses non-strict parsing (`.passthrough()` or Zod default behavior) so removing fields doesn't break users who have them
- IN: Remove the 4 dead fields from the schema
- IN: Remove corresponding type references if any
- OUT: Removing fields that ARE consumed (like `disabled_mcps`, `hashline_edit`, etc.)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/config/schema/oc-blackbytes-config.ts` â€” remove 4 field definitions

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Zod v4 `z.object()` by default strips unknown fields â€” verify this. If strict, need to add `.passthrough()` or `.strip()` to avoid breaking users with these fields in config
- These fields never had consumers â€” removal is cleanup, not deprecation
- Users with these fields in their config will see no change (fields were always no-ops)

**Risks/Gotchas:**
- If Zod schema uses strict parsing, users with these fields get validation errors. Must verify parsing mode first.

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- Existing tests pass
- Grep confirms no remaining references to removed fields
close_reason: Removed 4 dead schema fields

title: Remove unused permission-compat functions

**Context:** `permission-compat.ts` exports functions that are never called: `migrateAgentConfig`, `migrateToolsToPermission`, `createAgentToolAllowlist`. The denylist-to-allowlist migration is deferred.

**Scope:**

- IN: Keep `createAgentToolRestrictions` (it IS used)
- IN: Clean up imports/exports referencing removed functions
- OUT: Changing the remaining `createAgentToolRestrictions` function

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/agents/utils/permission-compat.ts` â€” remove 3 functions
- Any files importing the removed functions (verify with grep)

**Dependencies:** None

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- Grep confirms no remaining imports of removed functions
close_reason: Removed 3 unused functions

title: WS6: Misc Bug Fixes
description: Fix line count off-by-one and standardize SDK imports.
close_reason: All child tasks completed

title: Fix line count off-by-one in tool-execute-after-handler
description:
**Objective:** Fix incorrect line count for files with trailing newlines in write tool output summary.

**Context:** `tool-execute-after-handler.ts:162-163` computes line count as `content.split("\n").length`. A file with content `"hello\n"` splits to `["hello", ""]` = 2 lines, but it's actually 1 line.

**Scope:**
- IN: Fix line count at line ~163 to handle trailing newline
- OUT: Other changes to the handler

**Components Touched:**
- `src/handlers/tool-execute-after-handler.ts` â€” line ~163

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Correct formula: `content.endsWith("\n") ? content.split("\n").length - 1 : content.split("\n").length`
- Empty string case (`content === ""`) already handled (returns 0)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Fixed trailing newline off-by-one in line count calculation

title: Standardize SDK imports to @opencode-ai/sdk/v2

  - `src/extensions/agents/bytes/index.ts`

  - `src/extensions/agents/explore.ts`
  - `src/extensions/agents/general.ts`
- OUT: Changing `@opencode-ai/plugin` imports (different package)

**Components Touched:**
- The 6 files listed above â€” all importing `AgentConfig` type

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- All 6 files import `type { AgentConfig }` â€” these are type-only imports
- Verify `@opencode-ai/sdk/v2` exports `AgentConfig` before changing
- This is a simple find-and-replace: `from "@opencode-ai/sdk"` â†’ `from "@opencode-ai/sdk/v2"`

**Validation:**

- `bun run check` passes
- Grep confirms no remaining `from "@opencode-ai/sdk"` imports (without `/v2`)
close_reason: Standardized 6 files to @opencode-ai/sdk/v2

title: WS2: Glob Fallback Fix
description: Fix broken non-rg Unix fallback in glob module. The find fallback currently uses the grep binary path instead of the find binary.
close_reason: All child tasks completed

title: Create find binary resolution for glob
description:
**Objective:** Create a separate binary resolution module for the `find` command, used by glob's non-rg fallback path.

**Context:** `glob/constants.ts` re-exports `resolveGrepCli` from `../grep/constants`, which resolves to `rg` or system `grep`. Neither is `find`. When rg is unavailable, `glob/cli.ts:122-126` uses `cli.path` (the grep binary) with `buildFindArgs` syntax, which is wrong.

**Scope:**
- IN: Create `src/extensions/tools/glob/find-cli.ts` that resolves the `find` binary path
- IN: Export a `resolveFindCli()` function returning the path to the system `find` command
- OUT: Updating `glob/cli.ts` to use it (separate bead)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/tools/glob/find-cli.ts` (new file)

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- On Unix, `find` is at `/usr/bin/find` or discoverable via `which find`
- On Windows, the `find` fallback is not used (PowerShell path handles Windows)
- Keep it simple â€” no auto-install logic needed for `find` (it's a system utility)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Merged into ocb-6oi.2 â€” too trivial to be standalone

title: Update glob CLI to use find binary resolution
description:
**Objective:** Fix `glob/cli.ts` to use a dedicated find binary resolution instead of the grep CLI path when rg is unavailable.

**Context:** At `glob/cli.ts:122-126`, the else branch (non-rg, non-Windows) executes `buildFindArgs` with `cli.path` â€” but `cli.path` points to the grep binary, not `find`. The `buildFindArgs` logic itself is correct for `find` syntax; only the binary path is wrong.

**Scope:**
- IN: Create `src/extensions/tools/glob/find-cli.ts` with a `resolveFindCli()` function that resolves the system `find` binary path
- IN: Import `resolveFindCli` in `glob/cli.ts`
- IN: In the else branch at line ~122, use the find binary path instead of `cli.path`
- OUT: Changing `buildFindArgs` logic (it's already correct)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/tools/glob/find-cli.ts` (new file) â€” `resolveFindCli()` returning path to system `find`
- `src/extensions/tools/glob/cli.ts` â€” lines 104, 122-126

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- On Unix, `find` is at `/usr/bin/find` or discoverable via `which find`
- On Windows, the `find` fallback is not used (PowerShell path handles Windows)
- Keep it simple â€” no auto-install logic needed for `find` (it's a system utility)
- Only the binary path changes; the args and cwd logic remain the same
- The `resolvedCli` parameter is still used for the rg path; find resolution is separate
- Must not break the rg path or PowerShell path

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- Manual test: glob works when rg is not available (mock or rename rg binary)
close_reason: Created find-cli.ts and updated glob/cli.ts to use resolveFindCli()

title: WS1: Workspace Boundary Enforcement
description: Prevent bundled tools from reading/writing outside the project directory. Create a shared assertWithinWorkspace utility and integrate into all bundled tools (grep, glob, hashline-edit, ast-grep). Uses realpath for symlink resolution with new-file fallback.
close_reason: All child tasks completed - workspace boundary enforcement in all tools

title: Create workspace boundary utility
description:
**Objective:** Create a shared `assertWithinWorkspace(resolvedPath, workspaceRoot)` utility that validates any path is within the workspace root, resolving symlinks.

**Context:** Bundled tools (grep, glob, hashline-edit, ast-grep) receive user/agent-supplied paths with no validation against the workspace root. The workspace root (`directory`) is available in the tool execution context but never checked.

**Scope:**
- IN: Create `src/shared/utils/workspace-boundary.ts` with `assertWithinWorkspace` function
- IN: Export from `src/shared/utils/index.ts`
- OUT: Integration into specific tools (separate beads)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/shared/utils/workspace-boundary.ts` (new file)
- `src/shared/utils/index.ts` (add export)

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Use `realpath` to resolve symlinks before comparison
- For non-existent files (hashline-edit creates), validate the parent directory's realpath instead
- Handle edge cases: path equals workspace root (allowed for grep/glob), trailing slashes, double slashes
- Normalize both paths before `startsWith` comparison
- Return clear error messages so agents understand why the path was rejected
- The workspace `directory` parameter is always an absolute, canonical path

**Risks/Gotchas:**
- `realpath` throws on non-existent files â€” must try parent dir fallback, then fall back to `resolve` + `normalize` prefix check
- Path normalization must handle platform differences (though primary target is Unix)

**Validation:**
- Unit tests covering: traversal attempts (`../../etc/passwd`), absolute paths outside workspace, symlinks, path equals workspace root, non-existent file with valid parent, non-existent parent dir
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Implemented assertWithinWorkspace utility in src/shared/utils/workspace-boundary.ts with symlink resolution, parent dir fallback, and lexical prefix check. Exported from index.ts. Build and lint pass.

title: Integrate workspace boundary into grep and glob tools
description:
**Objective:** Add workspace boundary validation to grep and glob tool handlers so they reject paths outside the project directory.

**Context:** `grep/tools.ts` and `glob/tools.ts` resolve user-supplied paths via `path.resolve(dir, args.path)` without validating the result stays within the workspace. An agent could be tricked into reading files outside the project.

**Scope:**
- IN: Add `assertWithinWorkspace` call in `grep/tools.ts` after path resolution (line ~42)
- IN: Add `assertWithinWorkspace` call in `glob/tools.ts` after path resolution (line ~31)
- OUT: hashline-edit and ast-grep (separate beads)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/tools/grep/tools.ts` â€” add import and call after `searchPath` resolution
- `src/extensions/tools/glob/tools.ts` â€” add import and call after `searchPath` resolution

**Dependencies:** ocb-9xj.1 (workspace boundary utility must exist)

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- `ctx.directory` is the workspace root â€” use it as `workspaceRoot` parameter
- Path equals workspace root should be allowed (grep/glob searching entire project is valid)
- The check runs before CLI invocation, catching invalid paths early
- Catch the assertion error and return a user-friendly tool error

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Added assertWithinWorkspace to grep and glob tools after path resolution

title: Integrate workspace boundary into hashline-edit
description:
**Objective:** Add workspace boundary validation to hashline-edit executor for both `filePath` and `rename` parameters.

**Context:** `hashline-edit-executor.ts` uses `Bun.file(filePath)` and `Bun.write(rename, ...)` directly with no path validation. An agent could write to or rename files outside the workspace.

**Scope:**
- IN: Validate `filePath` in the executor before any file operations
- IN: Validate `rename` path if provided (line ~148)
- OUT: Other tools (separate beads)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/tools/hashline-edit/hashline-edit-executor.ts` â€” add boundary check before file read/write. The executor already receives `context: ToolContext` (line ~73) which has `context.directory` available (used at line ~125 for formatters). No changes to `tools.ts` needed.

**Dependencies:** ocb-9xj.1 (workspace boundary utility must exist)

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- hashline-edit creates new files â€” use parent dir fallback mode of `assertWithinWorkspace`
- Both `filePath` and `rename` are absolute paths from the agent
- The `delete=true` mode also needs boundary checking on the target file
- path-equals-workspace-root should NOT be allowed for hashline-edit file operations
- `context.directory` is the workspace root â€” already available in the executor, no threading needed

**Risks/Gotchas:**
- `rename` to a non-existent path in a non-existent parent dir â€” the boundary check must handle this gracefully without false positives

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Added assertWithinWorkspace checks for filePath and rename in hashline-edit-executor.ts

title: Integrate workspace boundary into ast-grep tools
description:
**Objective:** Add workspace boundary validation to ast-grep search and replace tools for the `paths` parameter.

**Context:** AST-grep tools in `ast-grep/tools.ts` pass `args.paths ?? [ctx.directory]` to the search/replace runner without validating paths stay within the workspace.

**Scope:**
- IN: Validate each path in `args.paths` array against workspace root before passing to `runSg`
- OUT: Other tools (separate beads)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/tools/ast-grep/tools.ts` â€” add boundary check for `args.paths` (line ~66) in both search and replace tool definitions

**Dependencies:** ocb-9xj.1 (workspace boundary utility must exist)

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- `paths` is an optional array; when not provided, defaults to `[ctx.directory]` which is always valid
- Each path in the array must be validated individually
- Paths may be relative or absolute â€” resolve against `ctx.directory` before checking
- path-equals-workspace-root should be allowed (searching entire project is valid)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Added assertWithinWorkspace validation for each path in args.paths for both ast-grep search and replace

title: WS4: Prompt Injection Fixes
description: Make runtime context injection idempotent and hashline-edit prompt conditional on tool availability.
close_reason: All child tasks completed

title: Add idempotency check to runtime context injection
description:
**Objective:** Prevent duplicate `<available_resources>` sections when `appendRuntimeContextToAgents` is called multiple times.

**Context:** `runtime-context.ts:91-103` appends the runtime context section unconditionally. If the config hook runs more than once, agents accumulate duplicate sections.

**Scope:**
- IN: Add `agent.prompt.includes("<available_resources>")` check before appending
- OUT: Changing the content of the runtime context section

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/agents/utils/runtime-context.ts` â€” add guard at line ~99

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Simple `includes()` check on the marker tag is sufficient
- The marker `<available_resources>` is XML-like and unlikely in user-authored prompts
- If marker is present, skip appending entirely (content is deterministic per config state)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
close_reason: Added includes('<available_resources>') guard before appending runtime context

title: Gate hashline-edit prompt sections on config
description:
**Objective:** Only include hashline-edit workflow instructions in agent prompts when `hashline_edit` is enabled in config.

**Context:** Agent prompts (bytes default/gpt/gemini, general) unconditionally include `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow`. When hashline_edit is disabled, these instructions are misleading.

**Scope:**
- IN: Make `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow` inclusion conditional based on `hashline_edit` config
- IN: Thread `hashlineEditEnabled` boolean through agent factory functions
- IN: Affects `bytes/default.ts`, `bytes/gpt.ts`, `bytes/gemini.ts`, and `general` agent prompt
- OUT: Changing prompt content itself

**Implementation Approach:**
The agent factory functions (e.g., `createBytesAgent(model: string)` in `src/extensions/agents/bytes/index.ts`) need a second parameter: `hashlineEditEnabled: boolean`. The factories call prompt builders (e.g., `createDefaultBytesPrompt` in `bytes/default.ts`) which use template literals with `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow` at specific line positions (e.g., line ~63 in default.ts). Replace unconditional `${SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow}` with a conditional: `${hashlineEditEnabled ? SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow : ""}`. The caller in `agent-config-handler.ts` (which calls the factories) has access to the plugin config and can pass `pluginConfig.hashline_edit !== false`.

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/default.ts` â€” conditional inclusion at line ~63
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gpt.ts` â€” similar conditional
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gemini.ts` â€” similar conditional
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/index.ts` â€” `createBytesAgent(model, hashlineEditEnabled)` signature change
- `src/extensions/agents/general.ts` â€” if it references hashline-edit section
- `src/handlers/config-handler/agent-config-handler.ts` â€” pass `hashlineEditEnabled` when calling factories

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- `hashline_edit` defaults to true (enabled) â€” prompt content unchanged for default config
- When disabled, omit the entire hashline-edit workflow section, do not leave empty markers
- Ensure no double blank lines from conditional omission (use trim or filter)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- With hashline_edit disabled: agent prompts do NOT contain hashline workflow text
- With hashline_edit enabled (default): agent prompts are unchanged
close_reason: Hashline-edit prompt sections gated on hashlineEditEnabled param

title: WS3: Config Loading Improvements
description: Surface config errors visibly and use correct config dir resolution for desktop/Tauri runtime context.
close_reason: All child tasks completed

title: Pass input.client to config dir resolution
description:
**Objective:** Fix `loadPluginConfig` to pass `input.client` to `getOpenCodeConfigDir()` so desktop/Tauri config directories are resolved correctly.

**Context:** `config/loader.ts:28-30` hardcodes `binary: "opencode"`, ignoring the `input` parameter entirely. Desktop resolution exists at `opencode-config-dir.ts` for binary `opencode-desktop` but is never triggered.

**Scope:**
- IN: Change `loadPluginConfig` to use `input.client` as the `binary` parameter
- IN: Default to `"opencode"` if `input.client` is not a recognized binary name
- OUT: Adding new binary types or detection heuristics

**Components Touched:**
- `src/config/loader.ts` â€” line 29, change `binary: "opencode"` to use `input.client`

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- `input.client` likely contains a string like `"opencode"` or `"opencode-desktop"`
- If unrecognized, default to CLI behavior (`"opencode"`)
- Must not break existing CLI behavior
- This is a best-effort improvement â€” full desktop detection may require OpenCode API clarification

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- Existing config tests pass
close_reason: loadPluginConfig now uses input.client for binary param

title: Add structured warning return for config errors
description:
**Objective:** Make `loadPluginConfig` return a structured result with warnings so invalid config produces visible feedback.

**Context:** Currently, invalid config is silently logged via the buffered file logger. Users have no way to see config errors without checking `/tmp/oc-blackbytes.log`.

**Scope:**
- IN: Change `loadPluginConfig` return type to `{ config: OcBlackbytesConfig; warnings: string[] }`
- IN: Populate warnings for: invalid JSONC, schema validation errors, missing config (info-level)
- IN: Update all callers to handle the new return shape
- OUT: Creating a new warning channel in OpenCode (not possible via plugin API)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/config/loader.ts` â€” change return type and add warning collection
- `src/index.ts` (line ~7) â€” caller: `const pluginConfig = loadPluginConfig(ctx)` â†’ destructure `{ config, warnings }`
- `test/config.test.ts` (lines ~80, ~91) â€” update test assertions to match new return shape

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Return shape: `{ config: OcBlackbytesConfig; warnings: string[] }`
- Callers decide how to surface warnings (`console.warn` as fallback)
- Must not break existing behavior â€” config still loads with defaults on error
- The plugin API may not have a proper warning channel â€” use `console.warn` as fallback

**Risks/Gotchas:**
- Changing the return type is a breaking internal API change â€” the 3 callers listed above must all be updated atomically

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- Existing tests pass (after updating assertions)
close_reason: loadPluginConfig returns { config, warnings } with structured error reporting
